articles.title: GARY GENSLER'S CONTENTIOUS REIGN OVER CRYPTO APPROACHES ITS TWILIGHT
articles.summary: SEC Chair Gary Gensler's controversial oversight of the crypto industry is nearly over. He will likely depart following the 2024 presidential election, though he could technically remain as commissioner until 2026. His tenure has been marked by aggressive enforcement actions, including a House-passed bill for digital asset regulations, and resistance to crypto-specific rulemaking leading to court battles and congressional pushback. Gensler's remaining agenda includes potentially finalizing crypto exchange and custody rules. His successor's approach will likely depend on the election outcome.

articles.title: SEC LISTS CRYPTO AS AN EXAMINATION PRIORITY IN 2025
articles.summary: The SEC's Division of Examinations will prioritize crypto, including Bitcoin and Ether ETFs, in 2025 because of the need to monitor the volatility and tech risks of the market. Despite potential leadership changes, the SEC plans to continue examining crypto-related activities to ensure investor protection and regulatory compliance.

articles.title: CHAINLINK ANNOUNCES PRIVATE TRANSACTIONS
articles.summary: Chainlink has introduced CCIP Private Transactions, allowing financial institutions to securely connect private blockchains to the multi-chain economy while maintaining data privacy. Australia and New Zealand Banking Group (ANZ) will pilot this capability for cross-chain settlement of tokenized real-world assets under Singapore's Project Guardian to demonstrate the potential for end-to-end privacy between blockchain networks.

articles.title: WORLD'S FIRST ZK-BACKED DIGITAL IDENTITY LAUNCHED IN BUENOS AIRES FOR 3.6M ELIGIBLE CITIZENS
articles.summary: Buenos Aires has launched the world's first ZK-backed digital identity, successfully granting 3.6M citizens access to government services and the ability to verify credentials without exposing personal data, thanks to blockchain and zero-knowledge(zk) cryptography. By integrating QuarkID into the miBA platform, a digital platform for accessing government docs, residents now gain self-sovereign control over their digital identities, with the government no longer having visibility into how or when these identities are used.

articles.title: AS STABLECOINS GAIN TRACTION, WHAT'S NEXT FOR CRYPTO'S 'KILLER USE CASE?'
articles.summary: Stablecoins have become a dominant use case in crypto ($177B market cap and ~$5.1T in transactions in H1 24), rivaling traditional payment systems like Visa. They offer investment opportunities through public companies, potential competition with money market accounts, and may drive the growth of blockchain networks like Ethereum.

articles.title: HOW BLOCKCHAIN CAN HELP FIGHT FAKE AI
articles.summary: Blockchain-based identity platforms like Civic and Worldcoin are potential solutions for verifying human authenticity online. These systems combine biometrics, zero-knowledge proofs, and other cryptographic methods to prevent Sybil attacks in token airdrops and facilitate proof of humanity. While privacy concerns around biometric collection remain prevalent, client-side storage and consent-based access address these issues.

articles.title: BRIDGE INVESTMENT MEMO
articles.summary: This investment memo from March 2022 describes Bridge, a stablecoin API platform, as a “crypto native American Express” that enables users to transact across any chain while settling in their preferred currency. It acknowledges the team's exceptional background and the massive potential market opportunity, though it notes the immediate need for payment intermediaries in crypto remains uncertain.

articles.title: SKY MULLS CHANGES TO BRAND IDENTITY, POTENTIAL RETURN TO MAKERDAO
articles.summary: Sky, formerly known as MakerDAO, is considering a return to its old branding after a token migration in September. Founder Rune Christensen presented three options for the protocol: stay as Sky, return to the original Maker vision, or rebrand to Maker but make small changes to the new USDS stablecoin and foster better subDAO alignment. Though there are concerns about SKY's utility, the USDS stablecoin has achieved over 1 billion in total supply and integrations with DeFi protocols like Aave.

articles.title: BITCOIN SELF-CUSTODY
articles.summary: Bitcoin self-custody is essential for individual security and the longer-term health of the network.

articles.title: WARRIORS ANNOUNCE PARTNERSHIP WITH COINBASE
articles.summary: The NBA's Golden State Warriors have announced a partnership with Coinbase to become its official cryptocurrency platform and blockchain partner.

articles.title: DAY 0 FOR LAVA NETWORK
articles.summary: Lava, a decentralized RPC provider, has turned on its revenue switch for network contributors and LAVA stakers.

articles.title: THE BENEFIT OF STATELESS CLIENTS
articles.summary: Stateless clients can validate blockchains without knowing the state.
